actionable_feedback: Open facility_location_1.xlsx in a spreadsheet-capable environment (or enable openpyxl) and confirm that all derived-metric formulas and bundle-total formulas are present and match the memo.

In the memo and workbook, explicitly state whether remediation purchase costs (O1/O2/O3) are one-time global bundle costs or applied per record; if either interpretation changes numeric results, show both variants.

Add a short numeric summary table (spreadsheet tab and memo appendix) showing base-case and each feasible bundle's computed totals (cost, adjusted shortfall, total known cost) so reviewers can quickly cross-check.
Recompute and document the algebraic steps used to produce each reported break-even threshold in the spreadsheet (cells containing the algebra) and reference those cells in the memo.

If programmatic verification is desired, include a CSV export of the computed summary table or enable workbook parsing by providing formulas in a parseable sheet or by ensuring openpyxl compatibility.

feedback_summary: The submission meets the artifact contract and the memo contains internally consistent analysis and sensitivity calculations. However, the workbook (facility_location_1.xlsx) could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable) so spreadsheet formulas, cell-level computations, and the memo's numeric thresholds were not independently verified. The memo also assumes remediation costs are applied as global bundle costs; that assumption should be made explicit in the workbook/memo.
